---
title: "Export tables"
subtitle: "`as_event_table()` turns feature objects into rows."
sidebar: start
---

## From object to table

Feature objects are the in-memory contract. Table helpers provide an interchange surface for modeling, inspection, and downstream joins.

```{python}
from natural_features.core.interchange import as_event_table
from natural_features.workflows import extract_features

result = extract_features(
    "The scene opens on a quiet room.",
    features=["text.tokenize"],
)
words = result.features["text.tokenize"]

table = as_event_table(words)
table.loc[:, ["onset_s", "offset_s", "duration_s", "label", "schema"]]
```

## What gets added

The table includes event timing, labels, confidence when present, object columns when present, and provenance columns copied from metadata.

```{python}
sorted(set(table.columns) & {
    "extractor_id",
    "extractor_name",
    "params_hash",
    "code_version",
    "model_revision",
    "schema",
})
```

## Refuses to

- Table helpers require pandas through the storage/docs dependency path.
- Event-level arrays in `extra` must have compatible lengths.

## Read next

[Media and optional backends](05-media-and-optional-backends.qmd)
